Dan 	S. 	KennedyÔÇÖs
CRAFTSMANSHIP
OF
One to Many
Selling Presentations
Training 	- 	2014
How to Assemble
The 	Most Effective
Group Selling Presentations
for In-Person Delivery or Media
┬ę 	2014/GKIC. 	ALL 	Right 	Reserved
Please 	read 	important 	notices 	on 	page 	1.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 1 of 171 --

Please 	note: 	Through-out 	the
presentation 	you 	will 	notice 	some
of 	the 	pages 	are 	numbered 	out-of-
sequence.
We 	have 	omitted 	general 	house-
keeping 	items 	relevant 	to 	those
only 	at 	the 	live 	event 	(egs.
sponsorship 	pitches, 	blank 	pages,
and 	past 	GKIC 	events).
This 	is 	also 	due 	to 	formatting
issues. 	We 	numbered 	the 	pages 	so
that 	they 	correspond 	to 	the 	pages
referenced 	in 	the 	CD/DVD 	for 	your
ease 	of 	use. 	Please 	rest 	assured
that 	you 	are 	not 	missing 	any
information.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 171 --

Before 	you 	write 	a
one 	to 	many 	presentation
and 	then 	perform 	or 	deliver
a 	one 	to 	many 	presentation,
you 	should 	be
an
architect
of
a 	great 	presentation.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 171 --

THE 	OPEN
PURPOSES
´âś 	To STOP the listener or viewer from actually OR
mentally ÔÇťclicking outÔÇŁ
´âś 	To ENGAGE the L/V and make him THINK
(Often beginning with ?ÔÇÖs they ask themselves)
´âś 	To FRAME THE DISCUSSION
´âś 	To SHORT-CIRCUIT INSTANT ÔÇťNEGATIVESÔÇŁ
´âś 	To CREATE 	POSITIVE 	INTEREST
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 171 --

THE 	OPEN
1: What Must Be ESTABLISHED?
WHO is talking to them?
1. 	Celebrity
2. Authority
3. Credibility
4. Affinity
5. Likeability
6. Role/Voice/Personality
Important Cautions:
´éĚThe better known they are (to the audience), the less of this you must
ÔÇťestablishÔÇŁ 	ÔÇô 	BUT donÔÇÖt assume or take for granted fame OR authority, OR
the audienceÔÇÖs ability to link that generally to your specific presentation.
Nutri-Systems shows them Marie Osmond and identifies her, but she also immediately
shows Before pic and says, ÔÇťI lost 55 poundsÔÇŽÔÇŁ
´éĚIn selling repeatedly to the same audience, you will tire of Telling Your
Story and be vexed by finding ÔÇťnewÔÇŁ ways to tell it 	ÔÇô 	but it is usually a
mistake to short-change re-establishing yourself for the purpose at hand
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 171 --

1: What Must Be ESTABLISHED?
WHY is he talking to them (now)?
ÔÇô 	because of
(egs.):
A current or impending event, opportunity
or danger (timing)
A new and amazing ÔÇťbreakthroughÔÇŁ, discovery, change in
order of things
Their 	circumstances, situation,
coming of a certain age
ÔÇťMad As HellÔÇŁ Position
(egs.: 	Trudeau 	ÔÇô 	What THEY DonÔÇÖt Want You To Know)
ÔÇťBlackie StoryÔÇŁ and assigned/adopted Mission
´éĚThis is ÔÇśReason Why AdvertisingÔÇÖ, applied
´éĚKeep in mind The Principle of Profound Importance
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 171 --

1: What Must Be ESTABLISHED?
WHY should they listen to him 	ÔÇô 	about this?
Relevance Link
Egs.:
Athlete/Coach = Champion: I know what it takes to win
Shaq for Icy Hot: I know pain
Aging Female Actress: I know what itÔÇÖs like to look in the
mirror and
Three best, add-on options:
a.) Dramatic Promise MADE PERSONAL
b.) 	UNIQUELY Qualified
c.) In possession of a SECRET no one else knows or
could/would reveal
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 171 --

1: What Must Be ESTABLISHED?
Why should they ÔÇťstay tunedÔÇŁ?
CAUTION: 	A common mistake made is to presume theyÔÇÖll
stay tuned because of prior relationship, ego 	ÔÇô 	your
dazzling brilliance, extreme need or any other reason
or to assume it, because they took the time to pre-
register, to show up, even to pay. THE ONLY SAFE
ASSUMPTION is: they are subject to loss minute by
minute by minute.
INSIDERÔÇÖS 	RULE 	OF 	THUMB: 	Divide 	the 	length 	of 	your 	presentation
by 	3 	if 	it 	is 	60 	minutes 	or 	less, 	or 	by 	6 	if 	it 	is 	60 	to 	180
minutes, 	or 	by 	8 	to 	10 	if 	it 	is 	over 	3 	hours. 	Those 	are 	your
time-frames 	in 	which 	to 	deliver 	a 	headline, 	message, 	and 	tease.
Egs.: 	a 	75 	minute 	presentation 	divides 	into 	12 	to 	13 	ÔÇťbitsÔÇŁ 	each
with 	a 	beginning, 	middle 	and 	non-end, 	link 	to 	next 	bit 	OR 	end
and 	start 	of 	next 	bit.
ELEMENTS 	TO 	ÔÇťSTAY 	TUNEDÔÇŁ:
Quick Promises 	ÔÇô 	3 to (max) 5
Personal Relevance 	ÔÇô 	ÔÇťIf You AreÔÇŽÔÇŁ
Provocative Question(s)
Aroused Curiosity
Breaking News
Warning of Threat or Danger
Specter of Loss
Benefit Summary Statement
Bribe(s)
Direct, Specific Instruction
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 171 --

The 	more 	of
what 	needs 	established 	that
relies 	on 	you
getting 	it 	established
(vs. 	having 	it
established 	for 	you),
the 	harder 	the 	sales 	job
that 	follows 	is 	going 	to 	be.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 171 --

2. Adapting to Nature of Audience
3. Putting The Required Items
In Order
#2 may have a lot to do with #3* 	ÔÇô 	examples:
a.): Difference between ÔÇťcoldÔÇŁ audience 	vs. ÔÇťwarmÔÇŁ
audience vs. members, customers, fans
b.): Difference between strangers to the idea vs. those
familiar with the idea
c.): Difference between quality of source
d.): Difference in age or other demographics
e.): Difference between an audience 	accustomed to being
sold via the media and way you are selling vs. an
audience for whom it is foreign
f): Difference between having them inside four physical
walls vs. CD/DVD vs. online 	ÔÇô 	and if inside walls, time of
day, what preceded, etc.
*You should 	never default to the same, one structure for every
presentation, and particularly for its Open, and beware anybody
who tries to ÔÇťtemplateÔÇŁ this for you.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 171 --

4: A Big Idea That ÔÇťSticksÔÇŁ
VITAL POINT: 	PRIMACY AND RECENCY 	ÔÇô 	egs.: given a chain
of a dozen or more random numbers or a list of random
words, most people can immediately recall the first
several and the last several. 15 minutes after the
fact, only the first 1 or 2, last 1 or 2. The middle
gets lost. So, good presentation architecture ÔÇťplantsÔÇŁ
a big idea at the start and reconnects to it at the
end.
Simplicity By Exclusion 	ÔÇô 	you have a lot of facts, features, benefits,
promises, etc., but for THE Big Idea you must be a ÔÇťmaster of
exclusionÔÇŁ, setting aside much of this and boiling it all down to
something between one word and one sentence. For example, my entire
Renegade Millionaire pitch is about: 	autonomy. My entire Magnetic
Marketing pitch is about: 	attraction, not pursuit. The Sleep Number
bed pitch is about: the sleep number. Then, we try to begin 	and end with
that same Big Idea.
Simplicity By Statement 	ÔÇô 	our very successful Gold By The Inch idea
was ÔÇśweekend wealthÔÇÖ, stated as: 	Turn $399 Into $3,990 Every Weekend.
A current control of mine (copy of entire example @ end of this
Manual) is: 	Make 	Big Money In Big Chunks. One of the most successful
ÔÇô 	and legally troubled 	ÔÇô 	in weight loss was HalbertÔÇÖs: 	Burn Off Body Fat
Hour By Hour 	ÔÇô 	Even When You Sleep. Such things lend themselves to
being both the Start and the Finish.
A Whack On The Side Of The Head 	- 	in MADE TO STICK*, they
call this Unexpectedness. A great Big Idea stuns with a revelation 	ÔÇô 	even
poor people in Okinawa out-live rich people in America (because of
their diet). 	Or one of my favorites: ÔÇťWhat If Everything YouÔÇÖve Been
Told About 	_____ Is Wrong?ÔÇŁ 	Or, one I wrote for a discount club years
ago: 	ÔÇťSorry, But Only 	Idiots 	Pay Retail.ÔÇŁ
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 171 --

Powerful Image / Word Picture 	ÔÇô 	the presenter of a ÔÇťget richÔÇŁ
proposition speaking while exiting a private jet and walking to a limo at
Open, exiting limo and boarding jet at End. A vivid word picture like
ÔÇťthe dumb woodsman who refused to sharpen his axeÔÇŁÔÇŽ 	(Phill
GroveÔÇÖs) ÔÇťgun against my headÔÇŁ.
Specificity 	ÔÇô 	people want definiteness, concreteness and certainty, and
this ÔÇťsticksÔÇŁ much better than vague concepts. Psycho-Cybernetics is a
personal development program delivering vague and unquantifiable
benefits 	ÔÇô 	healthier self-image, better self-esteem, less anxiety over
othersÔÇÖ opinions, greater self-confidence. Within it all, in MaltzÔÇÖ words,
there is 	a specific Big Idea: 	Everything Difficult For You Suddenly
Made Easy. 	Which is ÔÇťstickierÔÇŁ: 	Master Stock Market Investing 	or
Master Stock Marketing Investing In 30 Minutes A Day In Just 30 Days
or 	Master Stock Marketing Investing: Double Your Money Every 6
Months Or Less Without Risk 	ÔÇô 	Learn How In Just 30 Days?
Trigger AN Emotion 	ÔÇô 	End Of America 	ÔÇô 	ALARM. Occupy Wall
Street 	ÔÇô 	ANGER. How To Win Friends & Influence People 	ÔÇô 	DESIRE
FOR POPULARITY. Take This Job & Shove It 	ÔÇô 	I AinÔÇÖt Working Here
No More 	ÔÇô 	RESENTMENT, REBELLION.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 171 --

Recommended Resources
GREAT LEADS: The Six Easiest Ways To Start Any Sales
Message by Michael Masterson and John Forde
HOW TO WRITE A GREAT ADVERTISEMENT by Victor Scwab
MADE TO STICK: Why Some Ideas Survive and Others Die by
Chip and Dan Heath
FASCINATE by Sally Hogshead (and the entire Fascination
Marketing System that I collaborated with her on)
To 	Study
The OPENING 3 to 5 Minutes of TV infomercials
Currently airing shows 	ÔÇô 	Sat. and Sun. da-yparts
Past successful shows 	relevant to your category
YouTube, Jordan-Whitney
The opening 1 to 2 pages of bestselling / series mystery
authorsÔÇÖ novels
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 171 --

IDEAS THAT STICK, 	BY THE NUMBERS
My Marketing Triangle = 3
TedÔÇÖs 10 Foods Never To Eat
CoveyÔÇÖs 7 Habits OfÔÇŽ
IDEAS THAT STICK, 	BY ALPHABET
A-B-C: Always Be Closing
B.O.L.D.:
1: Does your advertising have a 	BIG 	idea?
2: Does your advertising make an 	OUTRAGEOUS
promise?
3: Does your advertising 	LINK 	to its audience?
4: Does your advertising include clear and specific
response 	DIRECTIONS?
<repeat>:
BIG 	idea
OUTRAGEOUS 	promise
LINK 	to audience
DIRECTIONS
Caution: 	LONG acronyms are self-defeating.
www.getwsodownload.com

Christian cross

commercials
´éĚCialis 	ÔÇô 	2 bathtubs.

-- 34 of 171 --

THE MIDDLE
PURPOSES
>To KEEP The L/V Long Enough 	To Turn Interest Into
Commitment 	ÔÇô 	so that not acting/buying is disappointing or
irritating
TOP SECRET! 	Most marketers and writers and virtually no
video or online video producers understand that this is
at least as important as any of the other purposes of
The Middle. And many donÔÇÖt even understand that it
exists! 	BEWARE IGNORANT FOOLSÔÇÖ PRESSURE TO SURRENDER
TO BREVITY 	ÔÇô 	instead scheme to ÔÇťbuyÔÇŁ as much L/V time
as possible, for time invested alone bolsters
commitment to buy.
>To GET ESSENTIAL SEQUENTIAL AGREEMENT so
the L/V CAN buy
ADVANCED TECHNIQUE: Determine and list every agreement
that must be reached with a prospect before they can
buy. The most basic list is (1) Need/desire for
ÔÇśthingÔÇÖ, (2) superiority of YOUR thing, (3) urgency 	ÔÇô
but you can and should be much more thorough and
complex than that.
>To CREATE EMOTIONAL ATTACHMENT to you &
what is 	being offered
>To RAISE & OVERCOME Doubts & Objections
>To ELIMINATE ALTERNATIVE SOLUTIONS 	& Set Up
The Offer
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 35 of 171 --

>To GET ESSENTIAL SEQUENTIAL AGREEMENT so
the L/V CAN buy
Example 	ÔÇô 	Home Fire Alarms 	ÔÇô 	full system or heat detectors
1. 	BASIC FACT: Your home, treasured valuable, family, your own life are
important to protect.
(Egs. of error here 	ÔÇô 	yakking about legacy estate planning or life insurance to someone disinterested in
passing assets to heirs)
VITAL FACTS:
2. Fire is an extremely prevalent*, unpredictable, not entirely preventable
danger. (Epic size and scope of the ÔÇťepidemicÔÇŁ). *Almost statistically certain
YOU will be victimized at least once in your lifetime 	ÔÇô 	you CANNOT
escapeÔÇŽonly a matter of when, not if
3. TodayÔÇÖs building materials, furnishings, fabrics, chemicals stored at home,
etc. make the danger ever- 	greater (facts, reasons and statistics that prove the
epidemic is getting worse). YOUR home is an explosion waiting to happen, a
scary freaking death trap! 	ÔÇô 	look, there are 97 different fire triggers in your
home. (Photos, video, walk-around 	ÔÇô 	the Fire Risk Audit) (Mfg. Labels with Fire
Warnings)
4. Fire takes a terrible human toll (horror stories, burn victims, orphaned
children) 	ÔÇô 	and you would do 	anything (as a responsible family leader) to keep
your family from suffering such horror
5. Fire can strike any time, day or night, with no warning*
(*Important agreement that you must return to at Close)
IDEAS THAT CONTRADICT EXISTENT KNOWLEDGE OR BELIEF:
6. Smoke detectors are a BIG LIE 	ÔÇô 	and 	ÔÇô 	your current fire safety measures are
woefully insufficient: you are in danger
7. Heat detection AND smoke detection AND the earliest warning of fire possible
are essential
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 36 of 171 --

8. SUMMARY 	ÔÇô 	FEATURING WHAT YOU DIDNÔÇÖT KNOW BEFORE, THAT
YOU DO NOW. To summarize Need & Desire for Remedial & Urgent Action. (In
live presentations, a good place for trial close.)
9. Uh-oh. Bad news. The various options available 	all 	have serious flaws
10. Whew! Praise God! Thank your lucky stars! There is 	A 	solution that offers
total, fail-safe protection
11. THIS solution is proven, reliable, credible, highly recommended, etc.
(Authority, Celebrity, Popularity, ÔÇťAbove All OthersÔÇŁÔÇŽ..)
12. ÔÇťI AM BECAUSE I OWN.ÔÇŁ You will feel smart(er), 	safe(r), more responsible,
pride, have bragging rights, be able to be the smartest guy in the room, be
looked up to by (and laid more or at least tonight by) your wife, etc. by buying
this 	ÔÇô 	now
13. Others feel the same way 	ÔÇô 	testimonials re-state key points
14. Second FULL Summary
15. It is (obviously) vital to own this solution 	ÔÇô 	and thereÔÇÖs no time to waste. (Fire
can strike any time, day or night, with no warning.) Good news: instant result.
(Temporary installation right now. Next best: FedEx overnight shipment.*)
16. Price is 	not 	subject to question, becauseÔÇŽ category of one/no other choice,
comparison done for you 	ÔÇô 	here are the numbers, etc. + payment terms make
this a no-brainer
*Huge business mistake: urgency of sales argument sabotaged and/or customer
satisfaction damaged by delayed fulfillment.
www.getwsodownload.com

>To CREATE EMOTIONAL ATTACHMENT to you &
what is being offered
Personal
Trust
Desire for Association
HeÔÇÖs singinÔÇÖ my tune 	ÔÇô 	mission, patriotism, philosophy
He gets me
Have a beer with
Be in a foxhole with
Coach my kids
Wish my kid was likeÔÇŽ
Have sex withÔÇŽ. Be married toÔÇŽ
Useful E-Factors
Love 	ÔÇô 	Sex 	ÔÇô 	Acceptance 	ÔÇô 	Respect 	ÔÇô 	Self-Respect
Popularity 	ÔÇô 	Social Status 	ÔÇô 	ÔÇťLike MikeÔÇŁ 	ÔÇô 	Cool (Tolerant; Progressive; Early
Adopter)
Intelligence 	ÔÇô 	ÔÇťIn The KnowÔÇŁ 	ÔÇô 	ÔÇťAmaze Your FriendsÔÇŁ 	ÔÇô 	ÔÇťLife Of The PartyÔÇŁ
Greed 	ÔÇô 	Wealth 	ÔÇô 	Power 	ÔÇô 	Autonomy
Fear/ Security 	ÔÇô 	Safety 	ÔÇô 	Protection (from Loss, Embarrassment, etc.)
Guilt 	ÔÇô 	Shame 	ÔÇô 	Regret/Future Regret // Peace of Mind
Pride 	ÔÇô 	Ability 	ÔÇô 	Achievement 	ÔÇô 	Admiration (Envy) Of Others
Goodness 	ÔÇô 	Honesty 	ÔÇô 	Responsible Behavior 	ÔÇô 	Compassion 	ÔÇô
Benevolence
Significance 	ÔÇô 	Reason For Being 	ÔÇô 	ÔÇťIs That All There Is?ÔÇŁ
Fear of Ill Health, Age, 	Death // Immortality // Legacy
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 38 of 171 --

IF 	I 	SHOW 	YOU 	THIS, 	HOW 	DO 	YOU 	REACT?
´éĚNO. You and I will NEVER see eye to eye or get along
´éĚOh no, there he goes again
´éĚIÔÇÖll try to overlook it and pay attention anyway
´éĚYES! YouÔÇÖre my guy. LetÔÇÖs mount up ÔÇśn ride.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 39 of 171 --

In 	THE 	MIDDLE, 	we 	present
Explanation and exposition
Dictionary definition: a comprehensive description
and explanation of an idea or theory
1. 	Necessary 	ÔÇô 	facts, figures, how it works, why it works
2. Reasons Why 	ÔÇô 	it, you, now
3. The ÔÇťX-FACTORÔÇŁ 	of the product or proposition
Proof of assertions 	ÔÇô 	key methods
´éĚScience / faux science
´éĚAuthority / borrowed authority / faux authority
´éĚOf importance/celebrity 	ÔÇô 	as seen in, as seen on, associated
with
´éĚSocial / peer proof, in aggregate
´éĚSocial / peer 	proof, in anecdotes, examples, case histories
´éĚVISUAL 	ÔÇô 	representative, symbolic
´éĚ(Dramatic) DEMONSTRATION(S)
´éĚOf superiority = inferiority of other alternatives
TECHNIQUE: 	MAKE 	A 	LIST 	OF 	EVERY 	ASSERTION 	being
made, 	that 	needs 	to 	be 	accepted 	ÔÇô 	then 	apply 	one
or 	more 	forms 	of 	proof. 	If 	you 	have 	a 	project
team, 	have 	different 	people 	do 	this 	separately,
then 	combine, 	to 	avoid 	missing 	anything. 	If
time/space 	ultimately 	restricts 	ability, 	rank
assertions 	in 	order 	of 	ÔÇťdifficulty 	of 	being
swallowedÔÇŁ, 	and 	omit 	the 	last 	dangerous.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 40 of 171 --

Human 	Interest 	Stories
Yours 	ÔÇô 	the sellerÔÇÖs
Origin/Reason Why/Turning Point
Before/After
Be-Like-Me
The ÔÇśStarÔÇÖ Testimonial
Answers 	to 	& 	Invalidation 	of 	Doubts
& 	ObjecÔÇÖs
Known in advance
If ÔÇśliveÔÇÖ, as they happen
By all of the above means/integrated it
By direct statement and counter
By ÔÇťdevilÔÇÖs advocateÔÇŁ 	ÔÇô 	style host
STRUCTURE NOTES: 	The Middle gets divided into either 3
or 4 segments within the segmentÔÇŽ
If 4:
#1 	ÔÇô 	Explanation and exposition
#2 	ÔÇô 	Proof
#3 	ÔÇô 	Human Interest Stories
#4 	ÔÇô 	Elimination of Objections
If 3, you must either combine #1 with #2, then #3, then
#4 OR combine #2+#3, so it is #1 + #2&#3 + #4.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 41 of 171 --

Some 	Special 	ÔÇśMiddleÔÇÖ 	Elements
In Their 	Habitat
ÔÇśUniqueÔÇÖ Physical Locations
egs. classic Dave Del Dotto Show w. John Davidson on beach in Hawaii
ÔÇśThe Garden PartyÔÇÖ Chat-Fest
The ÔÇśMan On The StreetÔÇÖ
egs.: Blu-Blockers, Orthotics
Live Audience, Call-In/Fake Call-In, Live Online Chat &
Q/A
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 42 of 171 --

How 	To 	Hold 	Interest 	In 	Long 	& 	Even 	Very 	Long
Presentations
In 	A 	Short 	Attention 	Span 	World
1: Best possible audience 	+ dispense praise for the
smart/committed ones and shame on the dumb/lazy ones
2: Most relevant message possible
3: 	Organization into 3-Act or 4-Act Plays, with segments
within each Act, so they get satisfaction as they go along,
with a series of complete yet connected startsÔÇŽ.not one l-o-
n-g story with one punch line at the end.
4: Periodic cliffhangers, teases and 	new headlines.
5: Mixed content: 	information, humor, story, demo, etc.
6: Mixed media: 	photos, cartoons, video, text
7: Enough speed and pace they must focus to keep up
(Mild frustration is better than wandering attention)
8: Momentum 	ÔÇô 	pace picking up during the presentation
9: 	Possibly 	break the presentation up 	into a series of
presentations (GKIC online launch ÔÇśsystemÔÇÖ)
10: Consider lying to them about elapsed and coming
amounts of time
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 43 of 171 --

7 	Things 	They 	Must 	Realize 	& 	Know
As 	A 	Result 	Of 	Your 	Presentation 	Before 	They
Will 	Buy 	From 	You
1: Appropriateness
What You Offer Meets Their Needs And Desires 	ÔÇô 	which means you
understand them, respect them, appreciate them and are ÔÇťfor themÔÇŁ
2: Differentiation
What You Offer is 	NEW, IMPROVED &/OR DIFFERENT 	from any other
prior attempt theyÔÇÖve made at satisfying the need or desire and/or anything
else out there.
3: Excitement
Your product is FUN or ÔÇťCOOLÔÇŁ or EXCITING to own or use and show
to peers or tell peers about. 	ÔÇťThe Pocket HoseÔÇŁ that rolls itself up is cool
and looks like itÔÇÖll be fun to use. Rosetta Stone makes learning a foreign
language seem easy and fun. With Nutri-Systems, you get 4 meals a day
including pizza and ice cream. 	NO Disney commercial ever shows people
standing in long lines.
4: They Are NOT The Only Idiot
Even if you are revealing a highly prized, carefully guarded secret, you
have to juggle that with 	lots of people already benefiting or profiting
from it and/or 	a current, crazed mob of people embracing it.
5: TheyÔÇÖre Getting ÔÇťA DealÔÇŁ
The price is a bargain, not just fair.
6: They WonÔÇÖt Fail, WonÔÇÖt Be Embarrassed or Frustrated, WonÔÇÖt Be
ÔÇťDisappointed AgainÔÇŁ And CanÔÇÖt Lose
People want CERTAINTY and SAFETY. Definitive promises. Younger
looking skin their friends canÔÇÖt help but notice in just seven days. If they are
buying from you in a category where they have made prior purchases and
are likely dissatisfied with the results of that purchase 	ÔÇô 	whether for a
vacation, a diet, an opportunity, whatever 	ÔÇô 	that fact shouldnÔÇÖt be ignored; it
should be acknowledged and resolved.
7: There Are Reasons They MUST Hurry or Do It Now.
www.getwsodownload.com

2: FOR WHAT PURPOSE(S)
a.) To present a point
(egs.: make a claim you canÔÇÖt)
b.) To prove a point
c.) To demonstrate a point
d.) To counter an objection
e.) To be a 	surrogate
f.) To match a specific listener/viewer
g.) To shame
3: WHEN // IN WHAT ORDER
?: beginning 	ÔÇô 	middle 	ÔÇô 	end
?: in commercial or only elsewhere
?: whoÔÇÖs on first
>: (neglected) opportunity to present
Extended Testimonials & Extra
Testimonials
4: WHERE
a.) 	In what media (in EVERY media)
b.) 	In introduction(s)
c.) 	In studio v. in habitat

Structure 	Of 	Promises
BIG Promises
Vs. SMALL Promises*
Learn the secrets of people who never get sick
Learn how to boost your immune system
Grow twice the garden for half the cost 	ÔÇô 	and make your
neighbors Green With Envy!
50 tips for a more bountiful 	garden
Foil even professional burglars and thieves so they can never
find your valuables
The 5 best hiding places in your home for your valuables
DIRECT Promises
Vs. IMPLIED Promises*
Live At Least 15 Years Longer Than Your ÔÇśLife ExpectancyÔÇÖ 	ÔÇô
and stay 	out of the hospital, too
Forbidden Cures! 	ÔÇô 	Amazing, Proven Home Remedies That Heal
Without Drugs Or Doctors
You Can Get Very Rich, Very Fast Too 	- 	If YouÔÇÖll Join Those ÔÇśIn
The KnowÔÇÖ About ÔÇśThe New Frontier In AmericaÔÇÖ
There Is A ÔÇťNew FrontierÔÇŁ In America 	ÔÇô 	and it is making some
who are ÔÇśin the knowÔÇÖ very rich, very fast
Make $4,000 A Day, Sitting At Your Kitchen Table In Your
Underwear
There Are People Making Amazing Incomes With A New
ÔÇśKitchen Table BusinessÔÇÖ
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 70 of 171 --

IMPLIED Promises Framed As Questions
YOU 	ÔÇô 	A Millionaire? 	ThereÔÇÖs a New Millionaire
Explosion going on in America right now. Even in
this terrible economy, more ÔÇťordinaryÔÇŁ men and
women became New Millionaires in 2013 than any
prior year. How can this be? 	Do they have a secret?
If you knew their secret, could you and would you
be willing to follow simple 1-2-3, A-B-C directions
and ÔÇťinvestÔÇŁ as little as one hour a day?
ÔÇťCan You Write A Simple Sales Letter? 	Answer yes
and you could be in demand, earning great money,
writing just a few hours a day, from anywhere in the
worldÔÇŽ
Has it occurred to you? 	ÔÇô 	there must be a better
way!
Have you ever noticed there seem to be some
people who can eat whatever they want, as much
as they want 	ÔÇô 	and never gain weight?
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 71 of 171 --

PRIMARY Promises
Vs. Secondary Promises
Sales Professionals: Put An End To ÔÇśCold
ProspectingÔÇÖ, Once And For All!
´éĚNow you can invest 100% of your 	time 	actually selling 	ÔÇô 	not
hunting for someone to sell to
´éĚYou can double or triple your 	income 	easily 	and 	automatically,
when youÔÇÖre able 	to stop wasting time prospecting
´éĚYouÔÇÖll be the 	envy of 	everybody else in the office when they
watch good, quality prospects literally line up to talk to you 	ÔÇô
without you ever lifting a finger, making a cold call, suffering
through another ÔÇśmixerÔÇÖ to get 	them
´éĚWith the stress of never quite knowing where your next prospect
is coming from 	ÔÇô 	or when 	ÔÇô 	erased, you can finally 	relax, 	enjoy
selling again, and have a legitimate and authentic 	positive
attitude
´éĚYour 	spouse, kids and friends 	will even notice the difference in
you!
GOOD 	RULE 	OF 	THUMB: 	Three 	to 	five 	secondary
promises 	for 	each 	primary 	promise
www.getwsodownload.com

STACKING Promises
´éĚYou get a 	(1) 	Marketing MESSAGE that is
magnetically attractive plus 	(2) 	the ability to target
the right MARKET certain to be most responsive
plus 	(3) 	ability to use the best MEDIA to deliver
your message to the market
´éĚ(1) 	Grow Rich 	ÔÇô 	(2) 	With Peace Of Mind
´éĚ(1) 	Win Friends & 	(2) 	Influence People
´éĚConquer The Chaos: How 	To 	(1) 	Grow A Successful
Small Business 	(2) 	Without Going Crazy
What It ISNÔÇÖT Is A Promise
ÔÇťWITHOUT Going Crazy
ÔÇťNOT hunting for
ÔÇťWhen youÔÇÖre able to STOP
ÔÇťerased
*Taken from: The Architecture of Persuasion by Michael Masterson

ÔÇťSTOP & SUMMARIZEÔÇŁ AT POINTS WITHIN THE
PRESENTATION

PROMISE AT A TIME + ALWAYS CONNECT EACH ONE TO
THE PRIMARY PROMISE

-- 77 of 171 --

Mistakes 	most 	often 	made
with 	The 	Middle
Too complicated 	ÔÇô 	too many ideas, too much information
Tops, 3 linked ideas in each of the 3 or 4 segments,
but better if 1 per segment
L/V gets ÔÇťlostÔÇŁ 	ÔÇô 	each idea presented not linked back to previous
one(s) and/or not reinforcing THE big idea.
Watch the Hurrycane┬« DRTV spot: problems 	ÔÇô 	solution: the revolutionary
Hurrycane: unique, better design 	ÔÇô 	safer 	ÔÇô 	all ground surfaces 	ÔÇô 	folds up
into purse 	ÔÇô 	made in America 	ÔÇô 	guaranteed.
Each of the six points links to the next, and if you look backwards at the
path, they makes just as much sense traveling backwards.
Too much information 	ÔÇô 	not enough EMOTION
They only need to 	know 	the minimum necessary to buy before they buy;
they need to be inspired, motivated, swept up by negative and positive
emotion to the greatest extent possible. Egs.: they donÔÇÖt have to know how
Proactiv penetrates deeper to prevent breakouts, they just need to know it
does and see a little scientific-appearing drawing
No fun
The buying experience is best ÔÇťlivelyÔÇŁ, entertaining 	ÔÇô 	without distracting,
enthusiastic. This is a person or people IÔÇÖd like to hang with.
Creating doubt rather than reassurance
Too much info can raise doubts. Excess lawyering-up can kill sales.
Weasel clauses in guarantees. Invisible type they canÔÇÖt read.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 78 of 171 --

BRIDGES OR SEGUES
& THE BRIDGE TO THE CLOSE
Segue: 	an uninterrupted transition
from one song, act, movie scene,
dissertation to another. (Perceived
Seamlessness)
Bridge: 	a structural device, to get
from one place to another
(Acknowledged Change)
How to get from end of idea to next idea 	ÔÇô 	some
best options
´éĚBRIDGE: Summary statement + Introduction of next
´éĚBRIDGE: Trial close, itÔÇÖs ÔÇťIFÔÇŁ, the reason for Idea ÔÇťBÔÇŁ
´éĚSEGUE: Mini-story that ties Idea ÔÇťAÔÇŁ to Idea ÔÇťBÔÇŁ
´éĚSEGUE: Mini-demo that ties idea ÔÇťAÔÇŁ to idea ÔÇťBÔÇŁ
´éĚSEGUE: Testimonial that ties idea ÔÇťAÔÇŁ to idea ÔÇťBÔÇŁ
How to get from one segment to next
BRIDGE: Summary + ÔÇťteaseÔÇŁ/headline 	ÔÇô 	itÔÇÖs perfectly okay to essentially
say, ÔÇťOkay that wraps up ÔÇśaÔÇÖ 	ÔÇô 	now hereÔÇÖs the next amazing thing youÔÇÖre
about to discover: <insert headline>ÔÇŽproceed with ÔÇťBÔÇŁ
BRIDGE: Change of scene or format
Tip: 	as 	you 	progress 	past 	2nd segment or 	multiple
ideas, 	you 	may 	want 	to 	insert 	stacking-summary
bridges
www.getwsodownload.com

/ 	from 	Middle 	to 	Close

Difference between a book on carpentry and a TOOL KIT

Been asked so many times for
Here is 	EVERYTHING weÔÇÖve built for you

For lead/permission response

instructions
Alternate choices
(An A or B. A free v. paid. Media option. Delivery option.)
How much info to request?
Reassurances v. reasons not to
No salesman will call. If this makes sense to you, it will be
YOUR choice to pick up the phone and call us
Urgency v. procrastination
Egs: IÔÇÖm giving away my book, absolutely free
OR
IÔÇÖm giving away my book, absolutely free, to the first 100 callers

No sales pressure will occur. At the end of the appt, one of 	# of
things will happen 	ÔÇô 	entirely at YOUR choiceÔÇŽ

the 	right 	place 	doing 	the 	right 	thing, 	you 	can

-- 115 of 171 --

A SpeakerÔÇÖs Or PresenterÔÇÖs ÔÇťTellsÔÇŁ
That The Audience ÔÇťReadsÔÇŁ
As Reasons NOT To Buy
Significant change 	ÔÇô 	voice, tone, body 	ÔÇô 	when moving from
ÔÇścontentÔÇÖ to ÔÇścommercialÔÇÖ. 	(Watch yourself on 	video and in the
mirror.)
Any hint of apology or being apologetic for selling
Self-disparaging humor when shifting into or during selling.
(DoesnÔÇÖt mean you canÔÇÖt have a little fun with it and even let a
savvy audience in on the game. But you canÔÇÖt let it be felt as if you
are ÔÇťcovering upÔÇŁ a bad thing.)
Disrespectful handling of your product
Hesitation or confusion 	(if you memorize no other part of a
presentation, you should memorize and practice the actual
ÔÇścommercialÔÇÖ/close)
Hurrying the pitch 	vs. the preceding presentation, as if you are
embarrassed by it and want to get it hidden away as quickly as
possible
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 116 of 171 --

Format 	Changes 	Throughout 	a 	Presentation
Monologue 	ÔÇô 	DTC / w. Audience
1-to-1 interview
1-to-Panel interview
Seminar / Power Points 	presentation
Physical demonstration 	ÔÇô 	in studio
Physical demonstration 	ÔÇô 	in field
Cut to video on monitor
Length 	& 	Breakdown 	of 	Presentations
INFOMERCIALS
On TV: 28.5 minutes
On DVD sent to home or office: 45 to 90
Online 	ÔÇô 	28.5 to 60
In 28.5:
1st Call 	To Action 	3 min.
2nd Call To Action 	2.5
End of Show CTA 	1.5
Leaves 21.5 divided by 3 segments
Long-Medium-Short:
8.5/7.5/5.5
Medium 	ÔÇô 	Long 	ÔÇô 	Short:
7/9/5.5
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 117 of 171 --

WEBCAST
3 or 4 hours
If 3 = 3 or 4 segments
If 4 = 4 or 5 segments
Egs. 	4 hours = 240 minutes
3 CTA breaks of 8 minutes each
+ CTA @ end of 8 leaves 208 minutes
4 Segments:
Medium 	ÔÇô 	Long 	ÔÇô 	Short 	ÔÇôShorter
55/65/45/43
PREVIEW SEMINARS
3 hours: 2 or 3 segments (I prefer 3)
Egs from SuccessTrak: 3 hours = 180 MIN
Short 	(no selling @ 1st break), Long, Short
40/10 Min Break/60/15 Min Pitch/15 Min Break/30/10 min. end
pitch if needed
SPEECHES TO SELL
90 MIN, NO BREAK
2 HRS., BREAK QUESTIONABLE (Net Gain Only 15 Min.)
3 OR 3.5 HRS, BREAK
3.5 OR 4 	ÔÇô 	POSSIBLY 2 BREAKS (1st Not Selling)
www.getwsodownload.com

If Using A 	Seminar or Studio Audience
´éĚVolunteers
´éĚRent-To-Order
CONSIDER DIVERSITY &/OR MATCH TO BUYERS
´éĚMore Than You Need
´éĚClear and Complete Instructions
´éĚAssigned Babysitter
´éĚExtra Wardrobe On Hand
´éĚMAKE THEM HAPPY
´éĚPresenter(s) Must ÔÇśPlay WellÔÇÖ With People
If 	Inexperienced Presenters
´éĚNO Prompter or Cue Cards 	ÔÇô 	better to have visible notes
´éĚDonÔÇÖt make impractical demands
´éĚLimited rehearsal
´éĚAllow extra time
If using Pro Presenters
´éĚDefine, agree to & meet or exceed their requirements
´éĚCreate a time and way to 	ÔÇťmake friendsÔÇŁ
´éĚKnow or sensibly guess their limitations
´éĚÔÇťMassageÔÇŁ beyond contract 	ÔÇô 	if so, get new signed release
´éĚBe respectful and pleasant, but not deferential
´éĚGood idea to have a ÔÇťbad guyÔÇŁ on your team
Edit
´éĚIf Live to Broadcast/Webcast, consider 	tape delay
´éĚLive-Switch
´éĚQuality Post-Production (4X to 8X ratio) 	ÔÇô 	from multiple
takes
´éĚGet more than one ÔÇśproductÔÇÖ/use

-- 120 of 171 --

Production Day(s)
´éĚAudio
´éĚStill Photography
´éĚÔÇťMaking OfÔÇŁ
´éĚSocial Media
´éĚPublicity
BIG CAUTIONS
DONÔÇÖT OVER-PRODUCE. Keep a tight 	reign on video
producers, on web designers, graphics people, etc. 	ÔÇô 	we are
NOT doing TV shows, movies, advertising or industrial
videos. 	We are selling stuff.
DONÔÇÖT BE TOO PERFECT & TOO SLICK. For most of your
purposes, believability and authenticity reign of supreme
importance. You arenÔÇÖt competing for Hollywood awards or
to impress peers.
DONÔÇÖT UNDER-PRODUCE. Different audiences have different
tolerances, but ultimately, they are watching this on a small
screen, and that is the same screen they watch other things
on for which they have expectations.
TV is a VISUAL media. ItÔÇÖs helped a lot by visual stuff
happening; by visual interest.
CONSIDER CRAFTING A ÔÇťSHOWÔÇŁ SO YOU CAN CHANGE
THE CTAÔÇÖS WITHOUT ALTERING THE SHOW.
TEST IF YOU CAN.
www.getwsodownload.com

Information

-- 122 of 171 --

THE MULTI-MILLION DOLLAR SECRET
OF PRE-EXISTING CONDITIONS
FACT 	OF 	LIFE: 	RESULTS 	VARY 	ÔÇô 	HAVING 	NOTHING 	TO
DO 	WITH 	THE 	EFFICACY 	OF 	THE 	SPEAKER 	OR
PRESENTER, 	THE 	PRESENTATION 	STRUCTURE 	OR
CONTENT, 	THE 	VENUE 	OR 	THE 	MEDIA. 	Losers 	use 	this
as 	an 	Excuse. 	Winners 	manage 	it.
IN 	YOUR 	HANDS: 	ONLY 	BY 	EXERTING 	CONTROL 	OVER
ÔÇśPRE-EXISTING 	CONDITIONSÔÇÖ 	CAN 	YOU 	FULLY 	PROFIT
FROM 	A 	GREAT 	PRODUCT, 	OFFER, 	PRESENTATION 	AND
PRESENTER. 	It 	is 	arrogant, 	stupid 	and 	lazy 	to
presume 	you 	can 	achieve 	maximum 	success 	by 	your
brilliance 	alone.
Key issues
(1)
WHO 	ÔÇô 	the source, nature and appropriateness 	of the audience
WHO 	ÔÇô 	the buying capability of the audience
WHO 	ÔÇô 	the buying willingness of the audience
1. CAN 	they buy?
2. SHOULD 	they buy?
3. WILL 	they buy?
4. CAN & WILL 	they buy ÔÇťhereÔÇŁ?
(by this means) (from this person)
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 123 of 171 --

(2)
HOW THEY THINK & 	FEEL
ABOUT THE PRESENTATION-SITUATION
(egs.)
´éĚWere they tricked, snuck up on or brought to presentation with
disclosure of purpose ie. 	resenting 	being sold, 	surprised 	at
being sold, 	OK with 	being sold, 	intending to 	make a buying
decision 	--- 	why they 	are there? 	And is there fair play?
´éĚTheir attitude toward and perception of 	the presenter,
company, product 	held in advance 	of the presentation
´éĚTheir sense of the presentationÔÇÖs importance 	ÔÇô 	egs.: is it
available any time and all the time, on demand or 	is it ÔÇťMust See
TVÔÇŁ at a certain date and time or by appointment? Is it a regular or
rare occurrence? It is an ÔÇťEVENTÔÇŁ? A History-In-The-Making
Event? 	Is it particularly timely? Is it related to GAIN or PAIN or
RISK/THREAT? Is it NEW 	ÔÇô 	a new scientific 	or moneymaking
breakthrough, a ground floor opportunity, etc.? Is it LIMITED 	ÔÇô 	is
there scarcity and/or exclusivityÔÇŽ.can anybody be ÔÇśin the roomÔÇÖ or
was I carefully chosen to be invited or must I qualify to be let in?
´éĚIf you are in an environment of ÔÇťimportance comparisonÔÇŁ 	ÔÇô
how can the consumer tell that you are more important (or more
interesting or more valuable or otherwise more deserving of their
intention) than the others?
www.getwsodownload.com

14. Placement of Sales Tables

16. Your End-of-Presentation Movement

YOUR MIND-SET ABOUT IT ALLÔÇŽ

About WHAT you are selling
About 	HOW you are selling it
About YOURSELF
About MONEY
(From The MARKETING YOUR SERVICES Letter, 6/14)
Do I 	actually 	believe that
enthusiasm imbues itself in
the words of copy, in the ink
on the page, and ultimately
oozes out when the envelope
is opened, to subliminally
influence the reader? 	That
the energy transferred
affects the outcome?

ÔÇśvibrationsÔÇÖ that come out of a
speaker in a room?

-- 130 of 171 --

The Multi-Million Dollar Secret
Of Pre-Existing ENEMIES
Every targeted group of consumers has certain people or kinds of
people they distrust, dislike, are (quietly) angry at, etc. Mark Cuban
stepped into a lot of shit with his remark about his own prejudices 	ÔÇô 	walking
at night on a street and seeing two black men in hoodies coming toward
him, he said he would cross the street. There, if encountering two white
ÔÇśskinheadsÔÇÖ covered with tattoos, heÔÇÖs go back to the other side. Me too.
And a pretty good-sized number of other people too. Racial issues do not
stand alone. Southerners are biased against Northerners, and a lot of Ivy
League college educated elites look down on Southerners, and on blue
collar, small town, mid-America folksÔÇŽ.who Obama famously described
disdainfully as ÔÇťclinging to their guns and Bibles.ÔÇŁ There is still plenty of
anti-Semitism. Need I go on? 	Further, most people believe they are
ÔÇśvictimsÔÇÖ of, at least, unfair treatment, or of ÔÇśconspiracies against
them.ÔÇÖ 	Michael Lewis assured himself a ton of media hype for his book by
proclaiming that Wall Street is a ÔÇťrigged game.ÔÇŁ
No great presentation fails to 	take advantage of this fact of human
nature.
Presenting to a group in person or to individuals who fit a definite
demographic/psychographic profile by media, you always want to include
and align yourself against that ÔÇśenemyÔÇÖ they have 	PRE-EXISTING 	ENMITY
TOWARDÔÇŽ.PRE-EXISTING 	DISTRUST OFÔÇŽ..PRE-EXISTING 	WORRY
ABOUT.
www.getwsodownload.com
